contexts.label: Calm this down
contexts.description: The child is overwhelmed, dysregulated, or emotionally flooded, but the parent is focused on lowering intensity first.

contexts.label: Stop unsafe behavior
contexts.description: The behavior, movement, words, or situation could become unsafe and needs a clear safety action now.

contexts.label: Repair afterward
contexts.description: The child is calm enough to reconnect, repair harm, and make a next-time plan.

age_bands.note: Use very few words. The adult manages the space and offers one simple body cue.
Use short, concrete words. Name the feeling, lower demands, and offer one next step.
Preserve dignity while giving clear support, space, or a simple plan.

age_bands.label: Teen
age_bands.note: Use respectful, direct language. Avoid chasing, cornering, lecturing, or power struggles.

show_another_phrase_rule.description: Rotate among approved cards with the same behavior_id, age_band_id, and context_id. Never call live AI.
show_another_phrase_rule.fallback: If a combo ever has fewer than two approved cards, hide or disable Show another phrase.

search_rule.description: Search/classification retrieves existing approved cards only. It must not generate new scripts or advice.
search_rule.hidden_search_terms_note: Hidden search terms may include words parents type, but displayed script fields must stay universal.

universal_language_rule.description: User-facing script fields must work for any household, child, bystander, school, public setting, or family structure without requiring the parent to translate relationship words during a hard moment.

cards.helpful_note: A short repair conversation works better than a long lecture. End with one specific plan for next time.

cards.say_this: All done now. Next time, I will help sooner.
cards.do_this: Reconnect before correcting. A very young child learns repair through guided practice.
cards.helpful_note: The goal is to reduce shame while increasing responsibility. Both can happen at the same time.

cards.say_this: I can see your body feels in danger even though I am here with you. I am here. Breathe out slowly and feel your feet.
cards.do_this: Lower your voice and reduce the next step to one action: sit, breathe out, drink water, or hold something soft.
cards.helpful_note: Regulation comes before reasoning. The fewer decisions the child has to make, the easier it is for the body to settle.

cards.say_this: This is a big feeling. We are going to make it smaller one step at a time.
cards.do_this: Stop explaining. Offer one regulating option and wait quietly for ten seconds before saying more.
cards.helpful_note: Do not say calm down or rush breathing. Start with a longer exhale and a steady voice.

cards.say_this: You do not have to solve it yet. First we help your body feel safe.
cards.do_this: Move the audience away if possible. Big feelings often get bigger when the child feels watched.
cards.helpful_note: The script works best when the adult’s body matches the words: slower voice, fewer movements, and no rushing to solve.

cards.say_this: I will help you, and I need bodies to stay safe. We are stopping right here.
cards.do_this: Act first, explain later. Move demands away, reduce stimulation, and help the body orient.
cards.helpful_note: Safety language should be calm and boring. Repeating one clear phrase is usually better than adding more explanations.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: This is too big to keep going. I am making the space safe now.
cards.do_this: Use one repeated safety phrase. Do not demand eye contact, apology, or reasons yet.
cards.helpful_note: Do not say calm down or rush breathing. Start with a longer exhale and a steady voice.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: I am not mad. I am keeping everyone safe first.
cards.do_this: Create space, lower noise, and remove the next demand until the child’s body is safer.
cards.helpful_note: This card is for stopping risk, not teaching the whole lesson. The lesson belongs after the nervous system settles.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: That feeling got very big. Now we can repair and practice what to do next time.
cards.do_this: Ask for one small repair and one next-time action. Keep it short enough to succeed.
cards.helpful_note: Repair is not the same as punishment. It reconnects the child to safety, responsibility, and a better next step.

cards.say_this: You were overwhelmed. We are safe now, and we can make a small plan.
cards.do_this: Practice the replacement once: asking for space, pointing, using a signal, or calling for help.
cards.helpful_note: A short repair conversation works better than a long lecture. End with one specific plan for next time.

cards.say_this: The feeling was allowed. The hard part was how it came out. Let us try a safer way.
cards.do_this: Help the child name the first body clue without turning the conversation into a lecture.
cards.helpful_note: The goal is to reduce shame while increasing responsibility. Both can happen at the same time.

cards.say_this: I can tell your body feels in danger even though I am here with you. We are not solving it yet; we are helping your body settle first.
cards.do_this: Give space without leaving emotionally. Say the line, step back, and let the child choose quiet, water, or a reset spot.
cards.helpful_note: Regulation comes before reasoning. The fewer decisions the child has to make, the easier it is for the body to settle.

cards.say_this: I am going to lower the pressure. You can breathe out slowly and feel your feet.
cards.do_this: Stop the problem-solving. Help the nervous system first with lower volume, fewer words, and more space.
cards.helpful_note: Do not say calm down or rush breathing. Start with a longer exhale and a steady voice.

cards.say_this: You are not in trouble for panic. We are going to slow this down together.
cards.do_this: Offer a nonverbal option if talking is making it worse: thumbs up/down, pointing, or writing one word.
cards.helpful_note: The script works best when the adult’s body matches the words: slower voice, fewer movements, and no rushing to solve.

cards.say_this: I am pausing this because we are not solving anything while panic is this loud. We can talk when bodies and voices are safer.
cards.do_this: Stop the interaction without debating. Move demands away, reduce stimulation, and help the body orient.
cards.helpful_note: Safety language should be calm and boring. Repeating one clear phrase is usually better than adding more explanations.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: I am not going to argue while this is unsafe. I am going to move demands away, reduce stimulation, and help the body orient.
cards.do_this: Use distance and repetition. Say one safety line, then stop adding new words.
cards.helpful_note: Do not say calm down or rush breathing. Start with a longer exhale and a steady voice.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: I care about what is happening, and I am stopping the unsafe part first.
cards.do_this: Keep exits and bodies safe. Get another calm adult if the situation is not settling.
cards.helpful_note: This card is for stopping risk, not teaching the whole lesson. The lesson belongs after the nervous system settles.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: That was a hard moment. Let us talk about what started it, what helped, and the first body sign that panic is starting.
cards.do_this: Talk through three pieces: what started it, what helped, and the first body sign that panic is starting.
cards.helpful_note: Repair is not the same as punishment. It reconnects the child to safety, responsibility, and a better next step.

cards.say_this: You do not have to be ashamed of the feeling. We do need a plan for how to handle it safely.
cards.do_this: Ask the child to choose a next-time signal or safe plan. Write it down if that helps.
cards.helpful_note: A short repair conversation works better than a long lecture. End with one specific plan for next time.

cards.say_this: We are on the same team now. Let us repair what needs repair and choose a next-time signal.
cards.do_this: Repair any impact first, then make the plan. Do not keep rehashing once the lesson is clear.
cards.helpful_note: The goal is to reduce shame while increasing responsibility. Both can happen at the same time.

cards.say_this: I can see your body feels in danger even though I am here with you. I am not going to push for answers right now. Take space, and I will stay available.
cards.do_this: Respect space while staying available. Do not follow, corner, lecture, or demand immediate answers.
cards.helpful_note: Regulation comes before reasoning. The fewer decisions the child has to make, the easier it is for the body to settle.

cards.say_this: We can pause the problem and lower the intensity first. You can breathe out slowly and feel your feet.
cards.do_this: Pause the conversation for a set amount of time. Keep the door open for reconnection without forcing it.
cards.helpful_note: Do not say calm down or rush breathing. Start with a longer exhale and a steady voice.

cards.say_this: I am going to keep my voice steady. You can take a minute before we try to talk.
cards.do_this: Lower stimulation and offer autonomy: space, water, a short walk nearby, or a quiet reset before talking.
cards.helpful_note: The script works best when the adult’s body matches the words: slower voice, fewer movements, and no rushing to solve.

cards.say_this: I am taking this seriously. We are not solving anything while panic is this loud.
cards.do_this: Prioritize risk reduction over authority. Move demands away, reduce stimulation, and help the body orient.
cards.helpful_note: Safety language should be calm and boring. Repeating one clear phrase is usually better than adding more explanations.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: I will talk with you when this is safer. Right now I am going to move demands away, reduce stimulation, and help the body orient.
cards.do_this: Do not try to win the moment. Create space, protect people, and get support if risk continues.
cards.helpful_note: Do not say calm down or rush breathing. Start with a longer exhale and a steady voice.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: I am not going to escalate with you. I am going to keep people safe and get support if needed.
cards.do_this: Name the pause, step back, and follow any household safety plan or emergency plan if needed.
cards.helpful_note: This card is for stopping risk, not teaching the whole lesson. The lesson belongs after the nervous system settles.
cards.safety_note: If symptoms are new, severe, or include fainting, chest pain, or trouble breathing, seek medical help.

cards.say_this: I am ready to talk when you are. We need to understand what happened and the first body sign that panic is starting.
cards.do_this: Invite ownership without shame: what happened, what was the impact, and the first body sign that panic is starting.
cards.helpful_note: Repair is not the same as punishment. It reconnects the child to safety, responsibility, and a better next step.

cards.say_this: I am not here to shame you. I do want us to repair the impact and name what support would help sooner.
cards.do_this: Make a concrete prevention plan together. Include what the teen can do and what support the adult will provide.
cards.helpful_note: A short repair conversation works better than a long lecture. End with one specific plan for next time.

cards.say_this: That was intense. Let us slow it down, own what needs owning, and decide what changes before it gets that big again.
cards.do_this: Keep the tone respectful and brief. Teens often shut down if repair turns into a courtroom.
cards.helpful_note: The goal is to reduce shame while increasing responsibility. Both can happen at the same time.

moments.label: Calm this down
moments.description: The child is overwhelmed, dysregulated, or emotionally flooded, but the parent is focused on lowering intensity first.

moments.label: Stop unsafe behavior
moments.description: The behavior, movement, words, or situation could become unsafe and needs a clear safety action now.

moments.label: Repair afterward
moments.description: The child is calm enough to reconnect, repair harm, and make a next-time plan.
